---
title: "Mallick Hossain"
subtitle: "Senior Financial Economist"
image: images/avatar.jpg
about:
  template: trestles  
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: ""
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/mallickhossain/
    - icon: github
      text: ""
      href: https://github.com/emallickhossain
    - icon: google
      text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com.my/citations?hl=en&user=22Vq08IAAAAJ
    - icon: envelope
      text: ""
      href: mailto:your.email@philadelphiafed.org
    - icon: file-pdf
      text: CV
      href: files/HossainCV.pdf
---

## Bio

I am a senior financial economist at the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia. My research interests are in consumer finance, health economics, and disaster risk and insurance. Previously, I worked as a Staff Economist for President Obama's Council of Economic Advisers and for the Census Bureau. 

*The views expressed here are my own and do not necessarily represent the views of the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia or the Federal Reserve System.*

## Recent Research

- [Not Cashing In on Cashing Out: An Analysis of Low Cash-Out Refinance Rates (Accepted at *Real Estate Economics*)](http://doi.org/10.1111/1540-6229.70031) (with Igor Livshis and Collin Wardius)
- [Pricing Protection: Credit Scores, Disaster Risk, and Home Insurance Affordability](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5026600) (with Joshua Blonz, Ben Keys, Philip Mulder, and Joakim Weill) was cited by the NYTimes in a recent [article](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2026/03/09/climate/home-insurance-credit-scores.html)!

[See all research →](research.qmd)
